title: Remote Access and Network Management Paths · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Provide secure operator, vendor, and user connectivity with recoverable in-band and out-of-band paths. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a least-privilege management access plan after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare site-to-site and client-to-site VPNs, clientless access, authentication, authorization, and route scope.
Choose split or full tunnel by traffic path, inspection, performance, privacy, DNS, and local-network risk.
Choose SSH, GUI, API, or console management with encrypted transport, identity, least privilege, and logging.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: network access management. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Net Inband Outofband trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare in-band and out-of-band management paths and preserve isolated recovery without creating an unmonitored bypass.

content.text: For Net Inband Outofband, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Net Inband Outofband precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed review case derived from this module: network access management. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Out-of-band management is physically unreachable to attackers by definition.

stimulus.title: Net Inband Outofband case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: network access management. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Out-of-band management is physically unreachable to attackers by definition.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare in-band and out-of-band management paths and preserve isolated recovery without creating an unmonitored bypass. Artifact under review: a complete first version of a least-privilege management access plan while every earlier competency remains active.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Net Inband Outofband, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Inband Outofband applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare in-band and out-of-band management paths and preserve isolated recovery without creating an unmonitored bypass.
